findings.explanation: Missing allowed-tools grants unlimited access (Bash, WebFetch, MCP, etc.). Skill legitimately needs Bash, Read, Write, Edit for local server and workflow. No malicious use detected in any file — risk is theoretical. Recommend adding: allowed-tools: Bash(node:*), Bash(scripts/*), Read, Write, Edit

findings.content: description: "You MUST use this before any creative work"
findings.explanation: Overly broad trigger activates on nearly any development task, expanding attack surface. Not malicious — enforces design-first workflow — but unnecessarily wide.
Option to bind to all interfaces, documented for remote/container environments. Default is 127.0.0.1 (safe). No risk in normal usage.
Link to author's repository in HTML template header. Informational, not executable.
